name: Diabetic Retinopathy - Anti-VEGF Medication Query
description: Tests ability to find DR patients via anti-VEGF intravitreal injections. Three main agents: ranibizumab (Lucentis), aflibercept (Eylea), bevacizumab (Avastin off-label). All used for proliferative DR and diabetic macular edema. Anti-VEGFs are highly DR/AMD-specific (also used in age-related macular degeneration, but not in our generated population).
prompt: Find patients prescribed anti-VEGF intravitreal injections used for diabetic retinopathy, including ranibizumab, aflibercept, or bevacizumab.

prompts.naive: Find patients on anti-VEGF for diabetes eye disease.
prompts.broad: Find patients prescribed anti-vascular endothelial growth factor (anti-VEGF) intravitreal injections used for proliferative diabetic retinopathy and diabetic macular edema — ranibizumab (Lucentis), aflibercept (Eylea), and bevacizumab (Avastin, off-label for ophthalmology).
prompts.expert: I need MedicationRequest resources for any anti-VEGF therapy. Use these RxNorm ingredient codes: 595060 (ranibizumab), 1232150 (aflibercept), 253337 (bevacizumab). Combine in a single comma-separated code search. Anti-VEGFs are highly retinal-specific — main cross-indication is AMD (also retinal disease).

metadata.algorithm_path_description: Patients with anti-VEGF intravitreal injection orders. High retinal-disease specificity.
